summary: Rough pre‑FM soundboard with occasional piano distortion, but a legendary 1971 performance featuring scorching Dark Star, The Other One and a killer NFA>GDTRFB>NFA sequence.
review: Despite the age‑worn pre‑FM board source – marked by dropouts, some piano muddiness and occasional distortion – this show is consistently praised as one of ’71’s finest.  Jerry’s guitar shines on Comes a Time, Cumberland Blues and Sugaree while the band rolls through a monster Dark Star into The Other One and an unforgettable NFA>GDTRFB>NFA jam.  The energy, seamless transitions and high‑in‑the‑mix rhythm section more than compensate for the technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance (top‑tier show) while noting mixed but tolerable recording issues; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pre‑FM board source with occasional tape dropouts, piano distortion/muddiness and some hiss.  Several listeners note the sound improves after the first few tunes and that the mix is still listenable; a few call it "mint" despite the flaws.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road > Not Fade Away
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight seamless transitions – especially Dark Star → Drums → The Other One → Me & My Uncle and the NFA>GDTRFB>NFA sequence – creating a high‑energy, flowing second set that feels like one continuous jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone described as "shining" on Sugaree, Cumberland Blues, Loser and Comes a Time; dominates the mix with fire and engaging stage banter.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is praised for being high in the mix, driving Dark Star and grooving throughout; many note his presence adds power to the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano is a love‑hate point – some reviewers complain of distortion and muddiness, others celebrate the honky‑tonk feel and its contribution to the vibe when it works.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as "driving" and "hard" especially during Dark Star and The Other One; transitions praised.
